key_departures.impact: Etienne was the unquestioned lead back, posting 1,107 rushing yards, 7 TDs, and 36 receptions for 292 yards in 2025 — 1,399 scrimmage yards and 13 total TDs. His departure leaves a clear void at the top of the depth chart. Bhayshul Tuten (rookie, 2025 4th-rounder) is the likely replacement, but carries significant risk as an unproven starter. DeeJay Dallas was re-signed as depth.

Lloyd was a 2025 second-team All-Pro who led the defense with 5 INTs (including a 99-yard pick-six off Mahomes), 81 tackles, and 7 PDs. He was the defensive signal-caller and the most dynamic off-ball LB in the league in 2025. His departure is a significant loss. In-house options include Ventrell Miller (2023 4th-round pick who outplayed Lloyd in 2024) and Yasir Abdullah. Dennis Gardeck's role may expand. The Jaguars reportedly anticipated this departure and declined Lloyd's 5th-year option before his breakout.

Newsome was acquired mid-season via trade from Cleveland for Tyson Campbell and draft picks. He played as a rotational CB, not a locked-in starter. His departure is manageable given the re-signing of Montaric Brown and the existing depth of Travis Hunter, Jourdan Lewis, Jarrian Jones, and Christian Braswell. The Jaguars were set to lose both starting outside CBs (Brown + Newsome) but addressed that by locking up Brown before the market opened.
Released for $2.7 million in cap savings. Was depth-only; Brenton Strange and Quintin Morris remain at TE.

Ogbah was a rotational edge rusher who recorded 12 tackles, 1 sack in 14 games in 2025. His departure as a UFA has minor impact given Josh Hines-Allen and Travon Walker anchor the position, but depth thins.
Veteran depth edge rusher. Departure has minimal impact.
Long-time Jaguars depth safety and core special teamer. Loses versatile ST piece but Antonio Johnson and Eric Murray return as starters.
Veteran IDL depth piece. Departure is low-impact given Matt Dickerson was re-signed and Maason Smith is developing.
Depth WR, minor impact. Brian Thomas Jr., Jakobi Meyers, and Travis Hunter handle WR duties.
Depth WR with 20 receptions in 2025. Minor loss; not a starter-level player.

contract.notes: Signed December 19, 2025 — mid-season extension. $30M fully guaranteed at signing. Extension runs through 2028 season. Counts as 2026 offseason retention.

signings.analysis: Meyers was acquired at the Nov. 4 trade deadline (cost: 2026 4th- and 6th-round picks) and immediately transformed the offense — JAX averaged 33.3 PPG in his 6 games vs. 22 before. The Jaguars locked him up on a 3yr/$60M extension ($40M gtd) in December, well before the FA market opened. At $20M AAV with $40M in guarantees, this is a legitimate starter investment. Meyers enters 2026 as the WR2/slot anchor alongside Brian Thomas Jr. and Travis Hunter. WR need is fully addressed at the starter level; depth WR remains a minor draft interest.

contract.notes: Guaranteed amount not publicly confirmed; estimated at ~$15–18M based on AAV and positional market. Signed March 9, 2026, hours before tampering period opened.

signings.analysis: Brown led the team with 12 PBUs in 2025, posted a 69.6 passer rating in coverage (6th-best among CBs with 200+ coverage snaps per NGS), and was the defense's most consistent corner. At $11M AAV on a 3-year deal, this is a legitimate starter-level investment in Campanile's zone-heavy scheme where Brown excels. With Brown locked in alongside Travis Hunter, the Jaguars have their two starting outside CBs under contract. CB is no longer a meaningful need. Jourdan Lewis and Jarrian Jones provide additional slot depth.

contract.notes: Confirmed 2-year, $12M deal per multiple sources including LWOS. Guaranteed amount not reported.

signings.analysis: Gardeck re-signs as a rotational pass-rush LB who finished 2025 with 2.5 sacks, 6 TFLs, 25 pressures (6th among all LBs). His role could expand following Devin Lloyd's departure to Carolina. However, Gardeck is 31, came off a torn ACL the prior offseason, and had only 39% of defensive snaps in 2025. This is a $6M/yr depth deal — not a replacement for Lloyd's every-down starter role. LB remains a significant draft need. Ventrell Miller and Jack Kiser/Yasir Abdullah will compete for expanded roles internally, but the void is real.

contract.notes: 1-year deal worth up to $3M per Jordan Schultz/NFL reporter. Re-signed in February 2026.

signings.analysis: Morris is a blocking-specialist TE who appeared in 14 games in 2025 with 6 catches for 55 yards. His primary value is as a pass protector and ST contributor. Brenton Strange is the true starting TE. Morris's 1-year deal is pure depth retention; it does not address any TE need. The Jaguars could still benefit from adding a receiving TE in the draft.

contract.notes: Re-signed February 16, 2026. Contract terms not publicly reported; presumed low-cost depth deal. Was a late-season addition (Dec 16, 2025 signing) with 2 rushes for 21 yards and 6 KR in 3 games.

signings.analysis: Dallas is a depth/special teams back with minimal carry work. His retention provides a veteran body behind Bhayshul Tuten, but does not address the lead-back void left by Etienne's departure. RB remains a priority need for the draft.

contract.notes: Re-signed February 16, 2026. Exact contract terms not reported; presumed low-cost depth deal.

signings.analysis: Dickerson stepped up as a key IDL rotational piece in 2025 with 4 starts and 10 tackles. His return provides interior depth continuity. However, with Arik Armstead's future uncertain (potential post-June 1 cut candidate at $19.4M cap hit) and the team's desire for more interior pass rush, IDL remains a need. Dickerson does not solve the starter-quality IDL question.

contract.notes: Re-signed February 19, 2026 as exclusive rights free agent. Minimum contract.

signings.analysis: 2023 6th-round pick who played in all 17 games with 14 tackles and 3 PDs. ERFA retention — standard cost-controlled move. CB depth maintained.

contract.notes: Re-signed February 20, 2026 as restricted free agent. Signed December 25, 2025.

signings.analysis: Late-season addition to the 53-man roster. RFA retention for CB depth. Minimal roster impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Travis Etienne departed for New Orleans on a 4yr/$52M deal. He was the unquestioned starter with 1,107 rushing yards and 13 total TDs in 2025. DeeJay Dallas (re-signed, depth) does not replace him. Bhayshul Tuten is the internal heir apparent but has never started an NFL game. RB is now a critical need — likely rounds 2–3 in the draft, or a premium external acquisition.

Devin Lloyd (2025 2nd-team All-Pro, 5 INTs, defensive signal-caller) signed a 3yr/$45M deal with the Panthers. This is an enormous void in Campanile's scheme. Dennis Gardeck's re-signing (2yr/$12M) is rotational/pass-rush depth — not a like-for-like replacement. Ventrell Miller is the top in-house option but unproven as an every-down starter. LB is now a top draft priority, likely targeted in rounds 1–3.

Montaric Brown re-signed on a 3yr/$33M deal (~$11M AAV) — legitimate starter investment. He led the team with 12 PBUs and posted an elite coverage grade in 2025. Combined with Travis Hunter (2025 #2 overall pick), the Jaguars now have two proven starting outside CBs under multi-year deals. Jourdan Lewis, Jarrian Jones, and Christian Braswell provide slot/depth continuity. CB is no longer a meaningful need; a Day 3 developmental CB is the most they'd need in the draft.

Jakobi Meyers locked up on 3yr/$60M ($40M gtd) extension in December — a true WR2 investment that resolved the mid-season acquisition into a long-term roster piece. Brian Thomas Jr. (2024 1st-rounder) remains, plus Travis Hunter (2025 #2 overall) will contribute at WR on a part-time basis. WR depth drops are manageable given the quality at the top. No pressing WR need for early draft capital.

No meaningful additions at IDL. Matt Dickerson re-signed as depth. Arik Armstead ($19.4M cap hit in 2026) is a potential post-June 1 cut that would create ~$14.4M in savings but leave a starter vacancy. DaVon Hamilton is also a potential cap casualty. Maason Smith (2022 2nd-round pick) is developing. IDL remains a priority need — particularly if Armstead is released in June. Draft emphasis likely rounds 2–4.

No additions or major losses at EDGE. Josh Hines-Allen and Travon Walker remain anchors. Ogbah and Smoot departed as rotational/depth players. Gardeck's re-signing adds some pass-rush flexibility on the LB/EDGE hybrid. Depth at EDGE is thin after the veteran losses, making Day 2/3 EDGE depth worth monitoring in the draft, but not an urgent need.

Andrew Wingard departed as a UFA (depth/ST). Antonio Johnson and Eric Murray return as starters. Caleb Ransaw (2025 3rd-round pick) missed his rookie year with a foot injury and is expected to contribute in 2026. S need is unchanged — rotational/depth S in mid-rounds of the draft remains a possibility.

No changes at OT during free agency. Cole Van Lanen was extended mid-season 2025 (noted in offseason context), locking in the LT spot. Walker Little (potential trade/cut candidate) remains a question. OT depth/developmental need is unchanged.

Patrick Mekari and Robert Hainsey both had contracts restructured to create cap space — both remain on the roster, but with cap manipulation adding void years that may create future problems. No IOL additions were made. IOL depth and long-term starter quality remain concerns for the draft.

key_additions.headline: WR2 anchor locked in — Meyers' mid-season extension eliminates WR as a draft priority after transforming the Jacksonville offense
Starting CB retained on multi-year deal — Brown's 12 PBUs and elite zone coverage grade secured alongside Travis Hunter, solidifying the outside CB tandem
Pass-rush LB retained as rotational piece — Gardeck's blitz role may expand with Devin Lloyd's departure, but he is not a like-for-like replacement
Blocking TE depth retained for another year on a prove-it deal

key_losses.headline: Franchise RB departs after 5 seasons — Etienne's 1,107-yard, 13-TD season in 2026 walks out the door, making RB the Jaguars' most pressing 2026 draft need
2025 2nd-team All-Pro LB signs with rival Panthers — Lloyd's 5 INTs and defensive signal-calling leave a massive void that elevates LB to a top-1 draft priority
Mid-season trade acquisition departs — Newsome's loss is softened by Montaric Brown's re-signing, maintaining the two-CB starter picture

draft_capital_notes: The Jaguars traded their 2026 first-round pick (received via Meyers trade context). They hold a 2026 2nd-round pick (No. 56 overall). Losing Etienne and Devin Lloyd as UFA departures, while not signing external free agents, positions the Jaguars to receive an estimated 3 compensatory picks in the 2027 NFL Draft. The Meyers trade cost a 2026 4th- and 6th-round pick.
